text: Standing outside this glassed-in meeting space, I feel like an observer at the threshold of activity and contemplation. The bright, sterile lighting and clinical materials foster a sense of detachment, as though I am both within and apart from what unfolds. The reflections on the glass speak to the layered realities of perception—what is seen, what is hidden, what is mediated. I am curious, yet slightly unsettled by the ambiguity of my place here, wondering if I am meant to engage or simply to watch.
